# Role Responsibility Template

Use this template to document a role on any OctoAcme project. Copy the section below into a project one-pager, roster, or README to record who owns what before execution begins.

---

## Role Name
<!-- e.g., Technical Lead, UX/UI Designer, Security Champion -->

## Role Summary
<!-- 1–2 sentences describing the core purpose of this role on the project. -->

## Primary Responsibilities
- <!-- Responsibility 1 -->
- <!-- Responsibility 2 -->
- <!-- Responsibility 3 -->
- <!-- Add more as needed -->

## Goals / KPIs
- <!-- Goal or measurable outcome 1 -->
- <!-- Goal or measurable outcome 2 -->
- <!-- Goal or measurable outcome 3 -->

## Typical Communication Cadence
- <!-- e.g., Daily standup, weekly design review -->
- <!-- e.g., Async via PR comments / Slack -->

## Escalation Path
- **Level 1:** <!-- Who this role escalates to first -->
- **Level 2:** <!-- Secondary escalation (e.g., PM or Product Lead) -->
- **Level 3:** <!-- Sponsor / executive escalation if needed -->

## Interaction Matrix

| Hands off to / receives from | Frequency | Channel |
|------------------------------|-----------|---------|
| <!-- Role A -->              | <!-- e.g., Daily --> | <!-- e.g., Standup / Slack --> |
| <!-- Role B -->              |           |         |
| <!-- Role C -->              |           |         |

## Onboarding Checklist
- [ ] Access to required tools and systems granted
- [ ] Introduced to key counterparts (PM, PdM, Tech Lead)
- [ ] Reviewed project one-pager, goals, and success metrics
- [ ] Responsibilities and acceptance criteria reviewed
- [ ] First tasks assigned in project board
- [ ] Communication channels joined (Slack, mailing lists, etc.)
- [ ] Escalation path and decision-making process understood
